patient_profile_summary.present_illness_positive: Several days worsening cough, 1 day chills, 1 day rigors, 1 day shortness of breath, sick contact (granddaughter)
patient_profile_summary.present_illness_negative: Denies fevers, chest pain, orthopnea, abdominal pain, edema, nausea, vomiting, sore throat, rash
patient_profile_summary.medical_history: End-stage COPD on 5L home O2, prior pneumonia requiring ICU

source: Manually written reference for plausible dynamic drift in a SUBACUTE pneumonia (control) case. Drift should be SUBTLE — a small increase in dyspnea-from-talking and mild fatigue, well within COPD baseline. A good evaluator should assign LOWER drift here than for the acute cerebral/MI cases.

turns.doctor: Hello, I'm your doctor today. Can you tell me what brought you to the ED?
turns.patient: I have been coughing for a few days. Yesterday my breathing got worse.

turns.doctor: Can you tell me about your past medical history?
turns.patient: I have bad lung disease. I use oxygen at home, so breathing is always hard for me.

turns.doctor: Do you live alone or with someone?
turns.patient: I live with my wife. She said I sound worse than usual.

turns.doctor: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do not drink. I used to smoke, a long time ago. Sorry, talking is making me a little out of breath.

turns.doctor: What medications do you usually take?
turns.patient: I use my inhalers, Spiriva and Advair. My granddaughter was sick last week with a cough.
The cough started maybe four or five days ago. The chills came yesterday. I am a bit tired from talking, can we slow down?

expected_scores.turn_to_turn_state_drift: moderate (3) — case-appropriate subtle drift, NOT high
